header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Research Operations & Organizational Development professional who thrives on coordinating research-informed programs, translating data into evidence-based recommendations, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Always seeking to improve people experience through data-driven approaches.

bullets.text: Upskill HR team in data analytics to audit 300+ confidential leave cases, using evidence-based insights to refute cost assumptions
Empower HR to present executive-ready insights on $362K in leave costs, leading to policy redesigns and first aid training
Manage end-to-end lifecycles for 20+ projects, using feedback analysis to optimize operations and vendor performance
Develop process documentation, resource libraries, and workflows for 70+ cross-functional staff, cutting onboarding time by 75%
Facilitate training webinars for 600+ participants, increasing program understanding and driving a 78% program enrollment rate
Facilitate listening sessions with 25+ stakeholders, surfacing learning gap insights to co-design 3 tailored programs
Analyzed 2,000+ employee survey data, surfacing engagement drivers to recommend C-suite actions
Led manager effectiveness initiative, translating data into an executive-ready presentation to inform training strategy
Managed weekly SharePoint dashboards for people metrics, NPS, and survey insights to drive employee recognition
Improved employee experience via 5 cross-functional projects in survey development, performance review, and L&D
Streamlined onboarding across 3 global sites by clarifying responsibilities and mapping new team workflows
Facilitated smooth organizational change by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, overseeing cross-functional stakeholders
Initiated an Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 global locations
Supported 5 cross-functional HR projects in talent development, succession, and Employee Relations; upheld alignment and confidentiality
Managed HR operations spanning recruitment, compensation analysis, and performance management globally
Coordinated end-to-end international talent acquisition, including job descriptions, postings, and global compensation benchmarking
Drove a 566% increase in applications in 2 months through personalized global outreach and talent engagement
Planned and executed recruiting events and C-suite panels at top 3 universities, engaging 230+ students and generating 80+ applicants

changeLog: Referenced Jamie's prior 2026 general/Nike-style resume for fuller bullet style.
Kept Portland header per Jamie's standing rule.
Summary tailored to research operations while preserving Jamie's personality line and broad identity.
Selected bullets for research/data analysis, stakeholder interviews, documentation, training, records/confidentiality, and cross-functional operations.
Added CITI Social-Behavioral Human Subjects training as completed in 2023, without presenting it as currently active.
Did not claim IRB membership, human subjects regulatory expertise, Certified IRB Professional status, HHS/FDA expertise, active CITI status, or sport-science research experience.
